codeReview.summary: The feature work correctly addresses the reported M1 regressions (worker lifecycle unlinking, pipeline persistence nil handling, and extract-state guardrails), but it introduces a blocking atom-leak risk in API handlers by converting untrusted route params with String.to_atom/1.

issues.description: GET /api/map/:space/:slot converts user-controlled `slot` path input using `String.to_atom/1`, creating unbounded atoms at runtime. Repeated requests with unique slot values can exhaust the BEAM atom table and crash the node.
PUT /api/map/:space/:slot repeats the same unbounded `String.to_atom/1` conversion on untrusted route input, preserving the atom-exhaustion crash vector.

Recipe loading now uses `String.to_atom/1` for recipe-provided values. This fixes `to_existing_atom` races but also permits atom growth from recipe content; safer bounded conversion (whitelist/validated mapping) would avoid long-term atom-table risk.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared docs and mission metadata still claim 34 API endpoints, but router currently defines 25 routes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md states "34 REST endpoints" while lib/bropilot/api/router.ex currently has 25 route declarations (lines 15-123). Worker handoff also flagged this mismatch.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md does not explicitly prohibit `String.to_atom/1` on externally controlled values; this likely contributed to unsafe conversions in request-path handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md coding conventions list API response formats and module layout but no atom-safety rule; lib/bropilot/api/handlers/project.ex:128 and :149 use `String.to_atom(slot_str)` from URL params.

sharedStateObservations.observation: integration-worker procedure requires writing tests for end-state changes, but this regression/fix run relied on manual/API flow checks and added no regression tests for the lifecycle bug class.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/integration-worker/SKILL.md step 3 says "Write tests for the desired end state"; handoff `tests.added` is empty despite runtime lifecycle fixes.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit d7250d0. Regression fixes appear effective, but the feature should not pass as-is due to a blocking atom-exhaustion vector introduced in map slot handlers via `String.to_atom/1` on user input.
